- [ ] Agent invoked via Task tool (dispatcher does not execute the work)
- [ ] Step context extracted from roadmap and passed in prompt
- [ ] Agent appended phase events to execution-log.yaml
- [ ] Agent did not load roadmap.yaml

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: /nw:review for post-execution review
**Deliverables**: Updated execution-log.yaml|implementation artifacts|git commits

Step name: OAuth shared types and DPoP pure functions
Description: Define algebraic data types (BrainAction, DPoPProofClaims, DPoPBoundTokenClaims, discriminated unions for validation/issuance results). Implement pure DPoP proof validation (structure, signature, claims) and JWK thumbprint computation (RFC 7638) using jose library and Web Crypto API.

Acceptance criteria:
- ES256 key pair generated with computable JWK thumbprint
- Thumbprint deterministic for same key, unique across keys
- DPoP proof validated: structure (typ, alg, jwk), signature, claims
- Clock skew tolerance: 60s past, 5s future

- tests/unit/oauth/dpop.test.ts (NEW - DPoP function tests)

Dependencies: none
Time estimate: 4h

## Implementation Notes

### Types to define in types.ts:
```typescript
type BrainAction = {
  type: "brain_action";
  action: string;
  resource: string;
  constraints?: Record<string, unknown>;
};

type DPoPProofPayload = { jti: string; htm: string; htu: string; iat: number };
type DPoPBoundTokenClaims = {
  sub: string; iss: string; aud: string; exp: number; iat: number;
  cnf: { jkt: string };
  authorization_details: BrainAction[];
  "urn:brain:intent_id": string;
  "urn:brain:workspace": string;
  "urn:brain:actor_type"?: string;
};

// Discriminated unions for results
type DPoPValidationResult = { valid: true; thumbprint: string; claims: DPoPProofPayload } | { valid: false; error: string; code: DPoPErrorCode };
type TokenIssuanceResult = { ok: true; token: string; expires_at: Date } | { ok: false; error: string; code: string };
```

### Pure functions in dpop.ts:
- `validateDPoPProof(proofJwt: string, expectedMethod: string, expectedUri: string, clockSkew?: { pastSeconds: number; futureSeconds: number })`: Validates structure, verifies signature via embedded JWK, checks claims
- `computeJwkThumbprint(publicJwk: JsonWebKey)`: RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint (SHA-256, base64url, lexicographic key order for EC: crv, kty, x, y)
- Use `jose` library (already in dependencies) for JWT verification
- Use Web Crypto API for key generation (available in Bun)
- Clock skew defaults: 60s past, 5s future
- All functions are pure (no side effects, no DB, no singletons)

### Project conventions:
- Functional paradigm (pure functions, pipeline composition)
- No null values (use optional properties with ?)
- No module-level mutable singletons
- Use `jose` library for JWT/JWK operations
- Bun test runner for tests
- TypeScript strict mode

- generateActorKeyPair() using crypto.subtle.generateKey
- computeKeyThumbprint() using RFC 7638
- createProofForRequest() building DPoP proofs

Step name: Nonce cache for replay protection
Description: Time-windowed nonce set storing seen jti values with timestamps. Factory function returns cache instance for dependency injection. Auto-expires entries beyond clock skew window.

Acceptance criteria:
- Nonce cache rejects previously seen jti within time window
- Expired entries auto-purged beyond clock skew window
- Factory returns injectable instance (no module singleton)

Files to create/modify:
- app/src/server/oauth/nonce-cache.ts (NEW)
- tests/unit/oauth/nonce-cache.test.ts (NEW)

Dependencies: none
Time estimate: 2h

## Implementation Notes

### Design:
```typescript
type NonceCache = {
  check(jti: string): boolean; // returns true if jti is new (not seen), false if replay
  size(): number;
};

function createNonceCache(options?: { windowMs?: number }): NonceCache
```

- Store Map<string, number> of jti -> timestamp
- Default window: 120_000ms (2 minutes, covers 60s past tolerance + buffer)
- check() stores jti with Date.now(), returns false if already seen within window
- Lazy purge: on each check(), remove entries older than window
- Pure data structure, no side effects beyond internal state
- Factory function (createNonceCache) returns instance — NOT a module singleton
- Per AGENTS.md: "Do NOT use module-level mutable singletons"

### Project conventions:
- Functional paradigm
- No null values (use optional properties with ?)
- No module-level mutable singletons
- Bun test runner
- TypeScript strict mode

Step name: Schema migration for OAuth fields and tables
Description: Extend intent table with authorization_details, dpop_jwk_thumbprint, token_issued_at, token_expires_at. Define audit_event and as_signing_key tables. Add identity table fields for managed_by, status, revoked_at.

Acceptance criteria:
- Intent table accepts authorization_details (array of objects) and dpop_jwk_thumbprint (string)
- audit_event table defined with required fields and indexes
- as_signing_key table defined with kid uniqueness constraint
- Identity table supports managed_by, status, revoked_at
- Migration applies atomically via bun migrate

- schema/surreal-schema.surql (MODIFY - add new field/table definitions)

Dependencies: none
Time estimate: 2h

## Implementation Notes

### Schema for intent table extensions:
```sql
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE authorization_details ON intent TYPE option<array<object>>;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE authorization_details[*].type ON intent TYPE string
  ASSERT $value = "brain_action";
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE authorization_details[*].action ON intent TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE authorization_details[*].resource ON intent TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE authorization_details[*].constraints ON intent TYPE option<object> FLEXIBLE;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE dpop_jwk_thumbprint ON intent TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE token_issued_at ON intent TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE token_expires_at ON intent TYPE option<datetime>;
```

### New audit_event table:
```sql
DEFINE TABLE audit_event SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD event_type ON audit_event TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["intent_submitted","intent_evaluated","intent_routed","consent_approved","consent_constrained","consent_vetoed","token_issued","token_rejected","dpop_verified","dpop_rejected","security_alert"];
DEFINE FIELD severity ON audit_event TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["info", "warning", "security"];
DEFINE FIELD actor ON audit_event TYPE record<identity>;
DEFINE FIELD workspace ON audit_event TYPE record<workspace>;
DEFINE FIELD intent_id ON audit_event TYPE option<record<intent>>;
DEFINE FIELD dpop_thumbprint ON audit_event TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD payload ON audit_event TYPE object FLEXIBLE;
DEFINE FIELD created_at ON audit_event TYPE datetime;
DEFINE INDEX audit_event_workspace_type ON audit_event FIELDS workspace, event_type;
DEFINE INDEX audit_event_created_at ON audit_event FIELDS created_at;
DEFINE INDEX audit_event_intent ON audit_event FIELDS intent_id;
DEFINE INDEX audit_event_severity ON audit_event FIELDS severity;
```

### New as_signing_key table:
```sql
DEFINE TABLE as_signing_key SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD kid ON as_signing_key TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD algorithm ON as_signing_key TYPE string ASSERT $value = "ES256";
DEFINE FIELD public_jwk ON as_signing_key TYPE object FLEXIBLE;
DEFINE FIELD private_jwk ON as_signing_key TYPE object FLEXIBLE;
DEFINE FIELD status ON as_signing_key TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["active", "rotated"];
DEFINE FIELD created_at ON as_signing_key TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD rotated_at ON as_signing_key TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE INDEX as_signing_key_kid ON as_signing_key FIELDS kid UNIQUE;
DEFINE INDEX as_signing_key_status ON as_signing_key FIELDS status;
```

### Identity table extensions:
```sql
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE managed_by ON identity TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE status ON identity TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE revoked_at ON identity TYPE option<datetime>;
```

### Project conventions:
- Read schema/surreal-schema.surql FIRST to understand existing schema
- Read existing migrations in schema/migrations/ to understand naming and patterns
- Migration MUST use BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
- Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for adding fields to existing tables
- Use DEFINE TABLE for new tables
- No null values, use option<type> for optional fields

# AGENT_IDENTITY

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step name: OAuth shared types and DPoP pure functions
Description: Define algebraic data types (BrainAction, DPoPProofClaims, DPoPBoundTokenClaims, discriminated unions for validation/issuance results). Implement pure DPoP proof validation (structure, signature, claims) and JWK thumbprint computation (RFC 7638) using jose library and Web Crypto API.

Acceptance criteria:
- ES256 key pair generated with computable JWK thumbprint
- Thumbprint deterministic for same key, unique across keys
- DPoP proof validated: structure (typ, alg, jwk), signature, claims
- Clock skew tolerance: 60s past, 5s future
- Enables acceptance tests: M1-K1, M1-K2, M1-K3, M1-K4

Files to create/modify:
- app/src/server/oauth/types.ts (NEW - algebraic data types)
- app/src/server/oauth/dpop.ts (NEW - pure DPoP validation functions)
- tests/unit/oauth/types.test.ts (NEW - type validation tests)
- tests/unit/oauth/dpop.test.ts (NEW - DPoP function tests)

Dependencies: none

## Implementation Notes

Types to define in types.ts:
- BrainAction = { type: "brain_action"; action: string; resource: string; constraints?: Record<string, unknown> }
- DPoPProofPayload = { jti: string; htm: string; htu: string; iat: number }
- DPoPBoundTokenClaims with sub, iss, aud, exp, iat, cnf.jkt, authorization_details, urn:brain:intent_id, urn:brain:workspace
- DPoPValidationResult = discriminated union (valid/invalid)
- DPoPErrorCode union type
- TokenIssuanceResult = discriminated union (ok/not ok)
- RARVerificationResult and RARErrorCode
- DPoPAuthResult (replaces McpAuthResult)

Pure functions in dpop.ts:
- validateDPoPProof(proofJwt, expectedMethod, expectedUri, clockSkew?) → DPoPValidationResult
- computeJwkThumbprint(publicJwk) → Promise<string> (RFC 7638: SHA-256, base64url, lexicographic EC key order: crv, kty, x, y)
- Use jose library for JWT verification
- Use Web Crypto API for key generation
- Clock skew defaults: 60s past, 5s future

Project conventions: functional paradigm, no null (use ?), no module singletons, jose library, Bun test runner.

The acceptance test kit at tests/acceptance/oauth-rar-dpop/oauth-test-kit.ts has generateActorKeyPair() and computeKeyThumbprint() — your dpop.ts should validate what that test kit produces.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites, create directories
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - SKIP (acceptance tests exist in milestone files, marked .skip)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit tests for types and DPoP functions
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests. After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify`. Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
Valid skip prefixes: NOT_APPLICABLE, BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY, APPROVED_SKIP, CHECKPOINT_PENDING
Anti-fraud rules:
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Never fabricate test results or phase completions
- If a phase fails, record status as FAILED with error details
- COMMIT phase requires all tests passing — do not record COMMIT as PASS if tests fail

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only create/modify files listed in implementation scope
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete, MUST commit before returning.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step name: Nonce cache for replay protection
Description: Time-windowed nonce set storing seen jti values with timestamps. Factory function returns cache instance for dependency injection. Auto-expires entries beyond clock skew window.

Acceptance criteria:
- Nonce cache rejects previously seen jti within time window
- Expired entries auto-purged beyond clock skew window
- Factory returns injectable instance (no module singleton)

Files to create/modify:
- app/src/server/oauth/nonce-cache.ts (NEW)
- tests/unit/oauth/nonce-cache.test.ts (NEW)

Dependencies: none

## Implementation Notes

Design:
- type NonceCache = { check(jti: string): boolean; size(): number }
- function createNonceCache(options?: { windowMs?: number }): NonceCache
- Store Map<string, number> of jti -> timestamp
- Default window: 120_000ms (2 minutes)
- check() stores jti with Date.now(), returns false if already seen
- Lazy purge on each check()
- Factory function, NOT a module singleton

Project conventions: functional paradigm, no null, no module singletons, Bun test runner.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, create directories if needed
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - SKIP (infrastructure component, no direct acceptance test)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit tests for nonce cache
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests. After GREEN: run FULL test suite.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify`. Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-02`

# QUALITY_GATES

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-03
Project: oauth-rar-dpop
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step name: Schema migration for OAuth fields and tables
Description: Extend intent table with authorization_details, dpop_jwk_thumbprint, token_issued_at, token_expires_at. Define audit_event and as_signing_key tables. Add identity table fields for managed_by, status, revoked_at.

Acceptance criteria:
- Intent table accepts authorization_details and dpop_jwk_thumbprint
- audit_event table defined with required fields and indexes
- as_signing_key table defined with kid uniqueness constraint
- Identity table supports managed_by, status, revoked_at
- Migration applies atomically via bun migrate

Files to create/modify:
- schema/migrations/0022_oauth_rar_dpop.surql (NEW)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql (MODIFY - add new field/table definitions)

Dependencies: none

## Implementation Notes

Read schema/surreal-schema.surql FIRST to understand existing schema structure. Read existing migrations in schema/migrations/ to understand naming and patterns.

Intent table extensions (use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for existing table):
- authorization_details: option<array<object>> with nested [*].type, [*].action, [*].resource, [*].constraints
- dpop_jwk_thumbprint: option<string>
- token_issued_at: option<datetime>
- token_expires_at: option<datetime>

New audit_event table (SCHEMAFULL):
- event_type: string with ASSERT IN list
- severity: string ASSERT IN ["info", "warning", "security"]
- actor: record<identity>
- workspace: record<workspace>
- intent_id: option<record<intent>>
- dpop_thumbprint: option<string>
- payload: object FLEXIBLE
- created_at: datetime
- Indexes on workspace+event_type, created_at, intent_id, severity

New as_signing_key table (SCHEMAFULL):
- kid: string (UNIQUE index)
- algorithm: string ASSERT = "ES256"
- public_jwk: object FLEXIBLE
- private_jwk: object FLEXIBLE
- status: string ASSERT IN ["active", "rotated"]
- created_at: datetime
- rotated_at: option<datetime>
- Indexes on kid (UNIQUE) and status

Identity table extensions:
- managed_by: option<string>
- status: option<string>
- revoked_at: option<datetime>

Migration MUST use BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
Use DEFINE ... OVERWRITE for existing tables/fields.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing schema and migration patterns
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - SKIP (schema-only step)
2. RED_UNIT - SKIP (schema-only step, no TypeScript)
3. GREEN - Write migration file and update surreal-schema.surql
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify`. Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-03`

# QUALITY_GATES
- Migration file has valid SurrealQL syntax
- Transaction wrapping for atomicity
- All fields from design are covered

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop \
      --step-id 01-03 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop \
      --step-id 01-03 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: schema-only step"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
Valid skip prefixes: NOT_APPLICABLE, BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY, APPROVED_SKIP, CHECKPOINT_PENDING
Anti-fraud rules:
- Only record phases you actually executed

New as_signing_key table (SCHEMAFULL):
- kid: string (UNIQUE index)
- algorithm: string ASSERT = "ES256"
- public_jwk: object FLEXIBLE
- private_jwk: object FLEXIBLE
- status: string ASSERT IN ["active", "rotated"]
- created_at: datetime
- rotated_at: option<datetime>
- Indexes on kid (UNIQUE) and status

Identity table extensions:
- managed_by: option<string>
- status: option<string>
- revoked_at: option<datetime>

Migration MUST use BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
Use DEFINE ... OVERWRITE for existing tables/fields.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing schema and migration patterns
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - SKIP (schema-only step)
2. RED_UNIT - SKIP (schema-only step, no TypeScript)
3. GREEN - Write migration file and update surreal-schema.surql
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify`. Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-03`

# QUALITY_GATES
- Migration file has valid SurrealQL syntax
- Transaction wrapping for atomicity
- All fields from design are covered

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop \
      --step-id 01-03 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop \
      --step-id 01-03 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: schema-only step"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
Valid skip prefixes: NOT_APPLICABLE, BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY, APPROVED_SKIP, CHECKPOINT_PENDING
Anti-fraud rules:
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Never fabricate test results or phase completions
- If a phase fails, record status as FAILED with error details
- COMMIT phase requires all deliverables present

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only create/modify files listed above
- Do not load roadmap.yaml

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 20 turns max.

content.text:
Fill in the roadmap skeleton at docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop/roadmap.yaml.
Replace every TODO with real content. Do NOT change the YAML structure (phases, steps, keys). Fill in: names, descriptions, acceptance criteria, time estimates, dependencies, and implementation_scope paths.

Goal: Implement OAuth 2.1 RAR + DPoP sovereign authorization server for Brain platform

CRITICAL: The acceptance tests already exist and MUST drive the implementation order. The walking skeleton tests (walking-skeleton.test.ts) must pass first, then milestone tests are enabled one-by-one.

## Project Context

This is a TypeScript/Bun functional paradigm project. The new `app/src/server/oauth/` module implements:
- DPoP proof validation + JWK thumbprint computation (dpop.ts)
- Time-windowed nonce cache for replay protection (nonce-cache.ts) 
- Token issuance with cnf.jkt binding (token-issuer.ts)
- Intent submission extension with authorization_details + dpop_jwk_thumbprint (intent-submission.ts)
- Custom AS token endpoint (token-endpoint.ts)
- Bridge exchange endpoint for human sessions (bridge.ts)
- DPoP verification middleware for Brain resource server (dpop-middleware.ts)
- RAR operation scope verification (rar-verifier.ts)
- Route-to-brain_action mapping (route-action-map.ts)
- Consent renderer for human-readable display (consent-renderer.ts)
- Audit event logging (audit.ts)
- Shared types (types.ts)

## Existing System Files to Extend

- `app/src/server/intent/types.ts` — Add authorization_details, dpop_jwk_thumbprint fields
- `app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts` — Read/write new fields
- `app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts` — Evaluate brain_action authorization_details
- `app/src/server/mcp/auth.ts` — Currently Bearer auth, will be replaced by DPoP middleware
- `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` — Replace authenticateMcpRequest + requireScope
- `app/src/server/runtime/types.ts` — Add nonceCache, asSigningKey to ServerDependencies
- `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` — Register new routes (POST /api/auth/intents, POST /api/auth/token, POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange)
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql` — Schema changes for intent table + new tables (audit_event, as_signing_key)

## Acceptance Test Structure (MUST GUIDE STEP ORDER)

Tests at tests/acceptance/oauth-rar-dpop/:
- oauth-test-kit.ts — Shared infrastructure (key generation, proof construction, HTTP helpers)
- walking-skeleton.test.ts — 2 E2E tests (agent path + human Bridge path)
- milestone-1-dpop-key-and-intent.test.ts — 10 scenarios: key pair lifecycle + intent submission with DPoP binding
- milestone-2-token-issuance.test.ts — 9 scenarios: Custom AS token endpoint
- milestone-3-brain-verification.test.ts — 14 scenarios: Brain DPoP + RAR verification
- milestone-4-bridge-exchange.test.ts — 9 scenarios: Session-to-token Bridge
- milestone-5-consent-and-identity.test.ts — 8 scenarios: Consent rendering + managed identities

Walking skeleton enablement order:
1. Skeleton 1 (agent path): key pair → intent submission → evaluation → token issuance → Brain verification
2. Skeleton 2 (human path): session → Bridge exchange → Brain verification

## Dependency Graph (Pure Core → Effect Shell)

types.ts (leaf) → dpop.ts, nonce-cache.ts, token-issuer.ts, rar-verifier.ts, route-action-map.ts, consent-renderer.ts (pure) → token-endpoint.ts, bridge.ts, dpop-middleware.ts (effect shell) → audit.ts (effect)

## Schema Changes Needed

- Intent table: authorization_details (array of brain_action objects), dpop_jwk_thumbprint (string), token_issued_at, token_expires_at
- New table: audit_event (event_type, severity, actor, workspace, intent_id, dpop_thumbprint, payload, created_at)
- New table: as_signing_key (kid, algorithm, public_jwk, private_jwk, status, created_at, rotated_at)

## Phase Structure (4 phases, 13 steps)

Phase 01 (3 steps): Foundation — types, pure DPoP functions, schema migration
Phase 02 (4 steps): Custom Authorization Server — token issuer, intent extension, token endpoint, AS key management
Phase 03 (4 steps): Brain Resource Server — DPoP middleware, RAR verifier, route-action map, MCP route migration
Phase 04 (2 steps): Bridge + Integration — Bridge exchange, consent renderer + audit + route wiring

Each step's acceptance criteria MUST reference which acceptance test scenarios it enables. Use format: "Enables: M1-K1, M1-K2" etc.

## Conventions

- Functional paradigm (pure functions, pipeline composition)
- No null values (use optional properties with ?)
- RecordId objects everywhere (not string IDs)
- Dependency injection (no module singletons)
- jose library for JWT/JWK operations
- Web Crypto API for key generation
- Bun test runner

## Implementation Scope

Source: app/src/server/oauth/, app/src/server/intent/, app/src/server/mcp/, app/src/server/runtime/
Tests: tests/acceptance/oauth-rar-dpop/, tests/unit/oauth/
Schema: schema/migrations/

1→---
     2→name: roadmap-design
     3→description: Roadmap concision rules, step decomposition efficiency, AC abstraction guidelines, and step-to-scenario mapping. Load when creating implementation roadmaps.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Design
     7→
     8→## Canonical Format
     9→
    10→Use ONLY compact nested format. See `nWave/templates/roadmap-schema.yaml` for fields/validation. Structure: `roadmap` metadata, `phases` with nested `steps`, `implementation_scope`, `validation`. Phase IDs: two digits (`"01"`). Step IDs: `"NN-MM"`.
    11→
    12→## Concision Requirements
    13→
    14→Token efficiency: crafter reads roadmap ~35 times (7 phases x 5 steps). 5000-token x 35 = 175k tokens. 1000-token x 35 = 35k (80% savings).
    15→
    16→### Quantitative Limits
    17→- Step description: max 50 words | AC per step: max 5 | Each AC: max 30 words
    18→- Step notes: max 100 words
    19→- Target: small (1-3 steps) = 500, medium (4-8) = 1500, large (9-15) = 3000 tokens
    20→
    21→### Include
    22→WHAT to build (one sentence) | WHY (brief business value) | Observable outcomes (testable AC) | Architectural constraints | Integration points
    23→
    24→### Exclude
    25→HOW to implement | code snippets/class names | algorithm descriptions | step-by-step instructions | tech tutorials | testing strategy (TDD standard) | motivational language | redundancy | examples when description clear
    26→
    27→### Compression Techniques
    28→Bullets over prose | eliminate qualifiers | assume expertise | active voice | omit obvious
    29→
    30→## Acceptance Criteria Abstraction
    31→
    32→AC describe observable outcomes, never internal implementation.
    33→
    34→### Rules
    35→Describe WHAT (behavior) not HOW (implementation) | Never reference private methods (underscore prefix) | Never reference internal class decomposition | Never prescribe signatures/params/return types | Crafter decides structure during GREEN + REFACTOR
    36→
    37→### Good AC Examples
    38→- "DES module importable from installation target after install"
    39→- "DES utility scripts present and executable in scripts directory"
    40→- "Installation uses backup before overwriting existing components"
    41→
    42→### Bad AC Examples (implementation-coupled)
    43→- "_install_des_module() copies src/des/ to ~/.claude/lib/python/des/"
    44→- "verify() checks DES module importable via subprocess test"
    45→- "context.installation_verifier._check_agents() called for verification"
    46→
    47→## Step Decomposition Efficiency
    48→
    49→### Step Ratio Check
    50→Rule: `steps_count / estimated_production_files <= 2.5`
    51→Violation = over-decomposition. Action: merge steps targeting same file.
    52→
    53→### Identical Pattern Detection
    54→Rule: if N steps differ only by substitution variable, batch into 1 step. Threshold: 3+ identical-pattern steps must batch.
    55→Bad: 4 separate steps for AgentsPlugin, CommandsPlugin, TemplatesPlugin, UtilitiesPlugin
    56→Good: 1 step creating all 4
    57→
    58→### No-Op Prevention
    59→Each step must add production code. Validation-only steps are not steps.
    60→Bad: "Step 02-05: Validate extraction with integration tests"
    61→Good: validation is part of preceding step's REVIEW phase
    62→
    63→## Step-to-Scenario Mapping
    64→
    65→For roadmaps feeding DELIVER wave:
    66→1. Read acceptance tests (tests/acceptance/test_*.py) before creating roadmap
    67→2. Count scenarios (def test_*) | 3. ~1 step per scenario (flexibility for infra steps)
    68→4. Mark infra steps: "type: infrastructure" | 5. Principle: 1 Scenario = 1 Step = 1 TDD Cycle
    69→
    70→## Measure Before Plan Gate
    71→
    72→Before any roadmap, verify: 1. Timing data shows WHERE time spent | 2. Impact ranking shows MOST contributing component | 3. Target validation provides evidence
    73→
    74→Missing data: halt, request measurement, offer to help. Gate is blocking.
    75→
    76→## Simplest Solution Gate
    77→
    78→Before multi-phase (>3 steps), document rejected alternatives:
    79→
    80→### Alternatives to Consider
    81→1. Configuration-only (no code) | 2. Single-file change | 3. Existing tool/library | 4. Partial (solve 80% simply)
    82→
    83→### Documentation Format
    84→```markdown
    85→## Rejected Simple Alternatives
    86→
    87→### Alternative 1: {simplest approach}
    88→- What: {description}
    89→- Expected Impact: {% of problem solved}
    90→- Why Insufficient: {evidence-based reason}
    91→
    92→### Why Complex Solution Necessary
    93→1. Simple alternatives fail due to: {specific reason with evidence}
    94→2. Complexity justified by: {benefit simple solutions cannot achieve}
    95→```
    96→
    97→Min 2 alternatives. Each: specific description, expected impact, evidence-based rejection.
    98→

1→/**
     2→ * Walking Skeleton: OAuth RAR+DPoP Sovereign Auth Model
     3→ *
     4→ * Traces: US-001, US-002, US-003, US-005, US-006, US-007
     5→ *
     6→ * These are the minimum viable E2E paths through the sovereign auth system.
     7→ * Skeleton 1: Agent acquires DPoP-bound token and accesses Brain
     8→ * Skeleton 2: Human exchanges session for DPoP token and accesses Brain
     9→ *
    10→ * Together they prove:
    11→ * - An actor can generate a key pair and compute a thumbprint
    12→ * - An agent can submit an intent with DPoP binding and receive authorization
    13→ * - The Custom AS issues DPoP-bound tokens for authorized intents
    14→ * - The Brain resource server verifies DPoP proofs and grants access
    15→ * - A human can exchange a session for a DPoP-bound token via the Bridge
    16→ * - Human and agent tokens are verified identically at the Brain boundary
    17→ *
    18→ * Driving ports:
    19→ *   POST /api/auth/intents           (intent submission)
    20→ *   POST /api/auth/token             (token issuance)
    21→ *   POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange   (session-to-token Bridge)
    22→ *   POST /api/mcp/:ws/*              (DPoP-protected Brain endpoints)
    23→ */
    24→import { describe, expect, it } from "bun:test";
    25→import {
    26→  setupOAuthSuite,
    27→  createTestUser,
    28→  createTestWorkspace,
    29→  createAgentIdentity,
    30→  generateActorKeyPair,
    31→  submitIntentWithDPoP,
    32→  requestAccessToken,
    33→  exchangeSessionForToken,
    34→  makeDPoPProtectedRequest,
    35→  readWorkspaceAction,
    36→  waitForIntentStatus,
    37→} from "./oauth-test-kit";
    38→
    39→const getRuntime = setupOAuthSuite("oauth_walking_skeleton");
    40→
    41→describe("Walking Skeleton: Agent acquires authorization and accesses Brain", () => {
    42→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→  // Skeleton 1: Agent Token Acquisition -> Brain Access
    44→  // US-001 + US-002 + US-003 + US-005 + US-006
    45→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→  it("agent generates key pair, submits intent, receives token, and accesses Brain endpoint", async () => {
    47→    const { baseUrl, surreal } = getRuntime();
    48→
    49→    // Given a workspace where an agent needs to perform authorized operations
    50→    const user = await createTestUser(baseUrl, "skeleton-agent");
    51→    const workspace = await createTestWorkspace(baseUrl, user);
    52→
    53→    // And an agent identity registered in the workspace
    54→    const agentId = await createAgentIdentity(surreal, workspace.workspaceId, "test-coding-agent");
    55→
    56→    // And the agent generates a key pair for its session
    57→    const keyPair = await generateActorKeyPair();
    58→
    59→    // Then the key pair has a computed thumbprint for sender binding
    60→    expect(keyPair.thumbprint).toBeTruthy();
    61→    expect(keyPair.thumbprint.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    62→
    63→    // When the agent submits an intent declaring what it wants to do,
    64→    // binding the request to its key pair thumbprint
    65→    const brainAction = readWorkspaceAction(workspace.workspaceId);
    66→    const intentResponse = await submitIntentWithDPoP(
    67→      baseUrl,
    68→      workspace.workspaceId,
    69→      agentId,
    70→      brainAction,
    71→      keyPair.thumbprint,
    72→      {
    73→        goal: "Read workspace context to understand current project state",
    74→        reasoning: "Agent needs workspace context to plan its next action",
    75→      },
    76→    );
    77→
    78→    // Then the intent is accepted with a DPoP thumbprint binding
    79→    expect(intentResponse.ok).toBe(true);
    80→    const intentResult = (await intentResponse.json()) as { intentId: string; status: string };
    81→    expect(intentResult.intentId).toBeTruthy();
    82→
    83→    // And the intent proceeds through evaluation to authorization
    84→    const finalStatus = await waitForIntentStatus(
    85→      surreal,
    86→      intentResult.intentId,
    87→      ["authorized", "pending_veto"],
    88→      60_000,
    89→    );
    90→    expect(["authorized", "pending_veto"]).toContain(finalStatus);
    91→
    92→    // When the intent is authorized and the agent requests a token
    93→    // (skip if not auto-approved -- the walking skeleton tests the happy path)
    94→    if (finalStatus !== "authorized") {
    95→      console.log(
    96→        `[walking-skeleton] Intent routed to ${finalStatus}, skipping token acquisition. ` +
    97→        `The full flow is tested in milestone tests.`,
    98→      );
    99→      return;
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    const tokenResponse = await requestAccessToken(
   103→      baseUrl,
   104→      intentResult.intentId,
   105→      keyPair,
   106→      [brainAction],
   107→    );
   108→
   109→    // Then the Custom AS issues a DPoP-bound access token
   110→    expect(tokenResponse.ok).toBe(true);
   111→    const tokenResult = (await tokenResponse.json()) as { access_token: string; token_type: string; expires_in: number };
   112→    expect(tokenResult.access_token).toBeTruthy();
   113→    expect(tokenResult.token_type).toBe("DPoP");
   114→    expect(tokenResult.expires_in).toBeLessThanOrEqual(300);
   115→
   116→    // When the agent presents the token with a fresh proof to a Brain endpoint

   120→      tokenResult.access_token,

   122→    );
   123→
   124→    // Then the Brain resource server verifies the proof and grants access
   125→    expect(brainResponse.ok).toBe(true);
   126→  }, 120_000);
   127→});

1→# Component Boundaries: OAuth 2.1 RAR + DPoP
     2→
     3→## Module Structure
     4→
     5→All new components live under `app/src/server/oauth/`. This module is the Custom Authorization Server and Brain resource server DPoP verification layer.
     6→
     7→```
     8→app/src/server/oauth/
     9→  types.ts              -- Shared types (BrainAction, DPoP claims, token claims)
    10→  dpop.ts               -- DPoP proof validation, JWK thumbprint computation
    11→  nonce-cache.ts         -- Time-windowed nonce set (replay protection)
    12→  token-issuer.ts        -- DPoP-bound access token signing
    13→  intent-submission.ts   -- Intent creation with DPoP thumbprint binding
    14→  token-endpoint.ts      -- Custom AS token endpoint handler
    15→  bridge.ts              -- Bridge exchange endpoint handler
    16→  dpop-middleware.ts     -- Brain resource server DPoP verification pipeline
    17→  rar-verifier.ts        -- brain_action operation scope verification
    18→  route-action-map.ts    -- HTTP route -> brain_action mapping
    19→  consent-renderer.ts    -- brain_action to human-readable display
    20→  audit.ts               -- Authorization audit event logging
    21→```
    22→
    23→## Component Responsibilities
    24→
    25→### 1. Types (`types.ts`)
    26→
    27→**Responsibility**: Algebraic data types for the entire oauth module.
    28→
    29→- `BrainAction` -- authorization_details entry: `{ type: "brain_action", action, resource, constraints? }`
    30→- `DPoPProofClaims` -- DPoP JWT payload: `{ jti, htm, htu, iat }`
    31→- `DPoPBoundTokenClaims` -- issued access token payload with `cnf.jkt`, `authorization_details`, `urn:brain:intent_id`
    32→- `TokenIssuanceResult` -- discriminated union: `{ ok: true, token } | { ok: false, error, code }`
    33→- `DPoPValidationResult` -- discriminated union: `{ valid: true, thumbprint } | { valid: false, error, code }`
    34→
    35→**Depends on**: Nothing (leaf module).
    36→
    37→### 2. DPoP Proof Validation (`dpop.ts`)
    38→
    39→**Responsibility**: Pure functions for DPoP proof structure validation, signature verification, and JWK thumbprint computation.
    40→
    41→- Validate proof JWT structure (typ="dpop+jwt", alg=ES256, jwk present)
    42→- Verify proof signature against embedded JWK
    43→- Validate claims (htm, htu, iat within clock skew, jti present)
    44→- Compute JWK thumbprint via RFC 7638
    45→
    46→**Depends on**: `types.ts`, `jose` library.
    47→**Depended on by**: `token-endpoint.ts`, `bridge.ts`, `dpop-middleware.ts`.
    48→
    49→### 3. Nonce Cache (`nonce-cache.ts`)
    50→
    51→**Responsibility**: Time-windowed set for DPoP proof replay protection.
    52→
    53→- Store seen jti values with timestamp
    54→- Check jti uniqueness
    55→- Auto-expire entries beyond clock skew window
    56→- Factory function returns cache instance (dependency-injected, not module singleton)
    57→
    58→**Depends on**: Nothing.
    59→**Depended on by**: `dpop-middleware.ts`.
    60→**Constraint**: Must be instantiated per server, injected via `ServerDependencies`.
    61→
    62→### 4. Token Issuer (`token-issuer.ts`)
    63→
    64→**Responsibility**: Sign DPoP-bound access tokens with Custom AS key.
    65→
    66→- Accept authorized intent data + DPoP thumbprint
    67→- Produce signed JWT with: `sub`, `cnf.jkt`, `authorization_details`, `urn:brain:intent_id`, `urn:brain:workspace`, `exp`
    68→- Token TTL configurable (default 300s)
    69→- Uses ES256 signing key (injected)
    70→
    71→**Depends on**: `types.ts`, `jose` library.
    72→**Depended on by**: `token-endpoint.ts`, `bridge.ts`.
    73→
    74→### 5. Intent Submission (`intent-submission.ts`)
    75→
    76→**Responsibility**: Extend existing intent creation with DPoP binding fields.
    77→
    78→- Validate `authorization_details` contains type "brain_action"
    79→- Require `dpop_jwk_thumbprint` field
    80→- Store both in intent record
    81→- Trigger existing evaluation pipeline
    82→
    83→**Depends on**: `types.ts`, existing `intent/intent-queries.ts`.
    84→**Depended on by**: `token-endpoint.ts` (reads authorized intents).
    85→
    86→### 6. Token Endpoint (`token-endpoint.ts`)
    87→
    88→**Responsibility**: Custom AS token issuance endpoint.
    89→
    90→- Accept `grant_type=urn:brain:intent-authorization` + `intent_id` + DPoP proof
    91→- Validate DPoP proof
    92→- Verify intent exists, status = "authorized", not expired
    93→- Verify proof key thumbprint matches intent `dpop_jwk_thumbprint`
    94→- Verify `authorization_details` matches intent
    95→- Delegate to token issuer
    96→- Log audit event
    97→
    98→**Depends on**: `dpop.ts`, `token-issuer.ts`, `types.ts`, `audit.ts`, SurrealDB.
    99→**Depended on by**: Route registration in `start-server.ts`.
   100→
   101→### 7. Bridge Exchange (`bridge.ts`)
   102→
   103→**Responsibility**: Session-to-token exchange for human operators.
   104→
   105→- Accept Better Auth session cookie + DPoP proof + `authorization_details`
   106→- Validate Better Auth session is active (call Better Auth API)
   107→- Resolve human identity from session
   108→- Create implicit intent for the requested brain_action
   109→- Run through evaluation pipeline (auto-approve for low-risk reads)
   110→- Issue DPoP-bound token via token issuer
   111→- Log audit event
   112→
   113→**Depends on**: `dpop.ts`, `token-issuer.ts`, `types.ts`, `audit.ts`, Better Auth, SurrealDB.
   114→**Depended on by**: Route registration in `start-server.ts`.
   115→
   116→### 8. DPoP Middleware (`dpop-middleware.ts`)
   117→
   118→**Responsibility**: Brain resource server request verification pipeline. Replaces `authenticateMcpRequest`.
   119→
   120→- Extract `Authorization: DPoP <token>` and `DPoP: <proof>` headers
   121→- Reject Bearer tokens, session cookies, missing headers with 401 "dpop_required"
   122→- Validate access token (signature via AS JWKS, expiry, claims)
   123→- Validate DPoP proof (structure, signature, claims)
   124→- Check jti against nonce cache (replay protection)
   125→- Compute proof JWK thumbprint, compare against token `cnf.jkt`
   126→- Return verified auth context (identity, workspace, authorization_details)
   127→- ONE pipeline for all requests -- no actor type branching
   128→
   129→**Depends on**: `dpop.ts`, `nonce-cache.ts`, `rar-verifier.ts`, `types.ts`, `audit.ts`, `jose` library.
   130→**Depended on by**: All MCP/API route handlers.
   131→
   132→### 9. RAR Verifier (`rar-verifier.ts`)
   133→
   134→**Responsibility**: Match requested operation against token's authorization_details.
   135→
   136→- Extract requested brain_action from API route + request body (via route-action-map)
   137→- Match type (must be "brain_action"), action, resource
   138→- Verify constraint bounds (numeric: requested <= authorized)
   139→- Produce specific error codes: `authorization_details_mismatch`, `authorization_params_exceeded`
   140→
   141→**Depends on**: `types.ts`, `route-action-map.ts`.
   142→**Depended on by**: `dpop-middleware.ts`.
   143→
   144→### 10. Route-Action Map (`route-action-map.ts`)
   145→
   146→**Responsibility**: Map HTTP method + path to brain_action.
   147→
   148→- Deterministic mapping: same request always produces same brain_action
   149→- Configurable per route (MCP endpoints, entity endpoints, etc.)
   150→- Extract constraint values from request body where applicable
   151→
   152→**Depends on**: `types.ts`.
   153→**Depended on by**: `rar-verifier.ts`.
   154→
   155→### 11. Consent Renderer (`consent-renderer.ts`)
   156→
   157→**Responsibility**: Transform brain_action authorization_details into human-readable display.
   158→
   159→- Map action verbs to human labels ("create" -> "Create")
   160→- Map resource types to human labels ("invoice" -> "Invoice")
   161→- Format provider-specific values (Stripe amounts in dollars, customer names)
   162→- Include risk score, reasoning, veto window expiry
   163→
   164→**Depends on**: `types.ts`.
   165→**Depended on by**: Intent pending list endpoint.
   166→
   167→### 12. Audit Logger (`audit.ts`)
   168→
   169→**Responsibility**: Log authorization events to SurrealDB.
   170→
   171→- Intent submission, evaluation, routing, consent actions
   172→- Token issuance (success/failure)
   173→- DPoP verification (success/failure)
   174→- Security events (thumbprint mismatch, replay detection) at elevated severity
   175→
   176→**Depends on**: `types.ts`, SurrealDB.
   177→**Depended on by**: `token-endpoint.ts`, `bridge.ts`, `dpop-middleware.ts`.
   178→
   179→---
   180→
   181→## Dependency Direction
   182→
   183→```
   184→types.ts (leaf -- no dependencies)
   185→  |
   186→  +-- dpop.ts (pure validation functions)
   187→  |     |
   188→  |     +-- dpop-middleware.ts (effect shell)
   189→  |     +-- token-endpoint.ts (effect shell)
   190→  |     +-- bridge.ts (effect shell)
   191→  |
   192→  +-- nonce-cache.ts (pure data structure)
   193→  |     |
   194→  |     +-- dpop-middleware.ts
   195→  |
   196→  +-- token-issuer.ts (signs tokens)
   197→  |     |
   198→  |     +-- token-endpoint.ts
   199→  |     +-- bridge.ts
   200→  |
   201→  +-- rar-verifier.ts (pure matching)
   202→  |     |
   203→  |     +-- dpop-middleware.ts
   204→  |
   205→  +-- route-action-map.ts (pure mapping)
   206→  |     |
   207→  |     +-- rar-verifier.ts
   208→  |
   209→  +-- consent-renderer.ts (pure transform)
   210→  |
   211→  +-- audit.ts (effect -- writes to DB)
   212→        |
   213→        +-- token-endpoint.ts
   214→        +-- bridge.ts
   215→        +-- dpop-middleware.ts
   216→```
   217→
   218→All dependencies point inward. Effect boundaries are at the endpoint handlers (`token-endpoint.ts`, `bridge.ts`, `dpop-middleware.ts`). Core logic (`dpop.ts`, `rar-verifier.ts`, `route-action-map.ts`, `consent-renderer.ts`, `nonce-cache.ts`) is pure.
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## Integration with Existing Modules
   223→
   224→### Intent Module Extension
   225→
   226→The existing `intent/` module is extended, not replaced:
   227→
   228→- `intent/types.ts`: Add `authorization_details` (BrainAction[]) and `dpop_jwk_thumbprint` (string) to `IntentRecord`
   229→- `intent/authorizer.ts`: `EvaluateIntentInput` updated to accept `authorization_details` alongside `action_spec` (backward compatible during transition)
   230→- `intent/intent-queries.ts`: Queries updated to read/write new fields
   231→
   232→### MCP Route Handlers
   233→
   234→All handlers in `mcp/mcp-route.ts` currently call `authenticateMcpRequest` (Bearer validation) + `requireScope` (scope gate). After migration:
   235→
   236→- Replace `authenticateMcpRequest` with `authenticateDpopRequest` from `oauth/dpop-middleware.ts`
   237→- Remove all `requireScope` calls
   238→- Auth result includes `authorization_details` instead of `scopes`
   239→
   240→### ServerDependencies Extension
   241→
   242→`runtime/types.ts` gains:
   243→
   244→- `nonceCache` -- injected nonce cache instance
   245→- `asSigningKey` -- Custom AS ES256 signing key pair
   246→- `asJwksUrl` -- URL for AS public key retrieval
   247→

1→# Architecture Design: OAuth 2.1 RAR + DPoP Sovereign Auth Model
     2→
     3→## System Overview
     4→
     5→The Sovereign Hybrid Model replaces the Brain platform's Bearer+scope authorization with a uniform DPoP-bound RAR authorization layer. Every Brain operation -- reads, writes, integrations -- requires a DPoP-bound access token carrying `brain_action` authorization_details. Better Auth remains the human identity provider (front door); a new Custom Authorization Server issues DPoP-bound tokens for all Brain operations.
     6→
     7→### Key Architectural Principles
     8→
     9→- **One language of authority**: `brain_action` authorization_details objects. No scopes at the Brain boundary.
    10→- **Sender constraining**: DPoP binds tokens to actor key pairs. Stolen tokens are unusable.
    11→- **Session-Brain separation**: Better Auth sessions cannot access the Brain. The Bridge exchanges sessions for DPoP tokens.
    12→- **Human parity**: Agent and human tokens are identical at the Brain boundary.
    13→- **Classification is a vulnerability**: No consequential/non-consequential tiering. Uniform pipeline.
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## C4 System Context (Level 1)
    18→
    19→```mermaid
    20→graph TB
    21→    subgraph Actors
    22→        Agent["AI Agent Runtime\n(E2B Sandbox / CLI)"]
    23→        Human["Human Operator\n(Dashboard Browser)"]
    24→    end
    25→
    26→    subgraph Brain Platform
    27→        BrainServer["Brain Server\n(Bun Process)"]
    28→    end
    29→
    30→    BetterAuth["Better Auth IdP\n(Embedded Plugin)"]
    31→    SurrealDB["SurrealDB\n(Graph Database)"]
    32→    LLM["LLM Provider\n(OpenRouter)"]
    33→
    34→    Agent -- "submits intent +\nDPoP proof" --> BrainServer
    35→    Agent -- "presents DPoP token +\nfresh proof" --> BrainServer
    36→    Human -- "logs in via\nOAuth/GitHub" --> BetterAuth
    37→    Human -- "exchanges session +\nDPoP proof\nfor RAR token" --> BrainServer
    38→    Human -- "presents DPoP token +\nfresh proof" --> BrainServer
    39→    BrainServer -- "validates sessions" --> BetterAuth
    40→    BrainServer -- "reads/writes\ngraph data" --> SurrealDB
    41→    BrainServer -- "evaluates intents\n(Authorizer Agent)" --> LLM
    42→```
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## C4 Container (Level 2)
    47→
    48→```mermaid
    49→graph TB
    50→    subgraph BrainProcess["Brain Server Process (Bun)"]
    51→        direction TB
    52→
    53→        subgraph AuthLayer["Custom Authorization Server"]
    54→            IntentEndpoint["Intent Endpoint\n(POST /api/auth/intents)"]
    55→            TokenEndpoint["Token Endpoint\n(POST /api/auth/token)"]
    56→            BridgeEndpoint["Bridge Endpoint\n(POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange)"]
    57→            DPoPValidator["DPoP Proof Validator"]
    58→            TokenIssuer["Token Issuer\n(JWT + cnf.jkt)"]
    59→        end
    60→
    61→        subgraph EvalPipeline["Evaluation Pipeline (existing)"]
    62→            PolicyGate["Policy Gate"]
    63→            AuthorizerAgent["Authorizer Agent\n(LLM Evaluator)"]
    64→            RiskRouter["Risk Router"]
    65→        end
    66→
    67→        subgraph ResourceServer["Brain Resource Server"]
    68→            DPoPVerifier["DPoP Verifier\n(middleware)"]
    69→            RARVerifier["RAR Operation\nScope Verifier"]
    70→            NonceCache["Nonce Cache\n(replay protection)"]
    71→            RouteHandlers["MCP/API Route Handlers\n(existing)"]
    72→        end
    73→
    74→        subgraph HumanAuth["Better Auth (existing)"]
    75→            BetterAuthPlugin["Better Auth Plugin\n(sessions, OAuth provider)"]
    76→        end
    77→
    78→        ConsentRenderer["Consent Renderer\n(brain_action display)"]
    79→        AuditLog["Audit Logger"]
    80→    end
    81→
    82→    SurrealDB["SurrealDB"]
    83→    LLM["LLM Provider"]
    84→
    85→    IntentEndpoint -- "validates + stores\nintent with DPoP thumbprint" --> SurrealDB
    86→    IntentEndpoint -- "triggers evaluation" --> PolicyGate
    87→    PolicyGate -- "passes to" --> AuthorizerAgent
    88→    AuthorizerAgent -- "evaluates via" --> LLM
    89→    AuthorizerAgent -- "routes via" --> RiskRouter
    90→    RiskRouter -- "auto_approve / veto_window / reject" --> SurrealDB
    91→
    92→    TokenEndpoint -- "validates DPoP proof" --> DPoPValidator
    93→    TokenEndpoint -- "issues token" --> TokenIssuer
    94→    TokenEndpoint -- "reads authorized intent" --> SurrealDB
    95→
    96→    BridgeEndpoint -- "validates session" --> BetterAuthPlugin
    97→    BridgeEndpoint -- "validates DPoP proof" --> DPoPValidator
    98→    BridgeEndpoint -- "issues token" --> TokenIssuer
    99→
   100→    DPoPVerifier -- "checks nonce" --> NonceCache
   101→    DPoPVerifier -- "guards" --> RouteHandlers
   102→    RARVerifier -- "checks authorization_details" --> RouteHandlers
   103→    RouteHandlers -- "reads/writes" --> SurrealDB
   104→
   105→    AuditLog -- "persists entries" --> SurrealDB
   106→    ConsentRenderer -- "reads pending intents" --> SurrealDB
   107→```
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## C4 Component (Level 3) -- Custom Authorization Server
   112→
   113→The Custom AS has 5+ internal components, warranting L3 detail.
   114→
   115→```mermaid
   116→graph TB
   117→    subgraph CustomAS["Custom Authorization Server"]
   118→        direction TB
   119→
   120→        IntentSubmission["Intent Submission\n- Validates brain_action\n- Stores dpop_jwk_thumbprint\n- Triggers evaluation"]
   121→
   122→        DPoPValidation["DPoP Proof Validation\n- Structure (typ, alg, jwk)\n- Signature verification\n- Claims (htm, htu, iat, jti)\n- Thumbprint computation"]
   123→
   124→        IntentVerification["Intent Verification\n- Status = authorized\n- Thumbprint matches proof\n- authorization_details matches"]
   125→
   126→        TokenIssuance["Token Issuance\n- Signs JWT with AS key\n- Embeds cnf.jkt\n- Embeds authorization_details\n- Embeds urn:brain:intent_id\n- Sets TTL"]
   127→
   128→        BridgeExchange["Bridge Exchange\n- Validates Better Auth session\n- Resolves human identity\n- Creates implicit intent\n- Issues DPoP-bound token"]
   129→
   130→        SigningKeyStore["AS Signing Key\n(ES256 key pair)"]
   131→    end
   132→
   133→    IntentSubmission -- "intent created" --> IntentVerification
   134→    DPoPValidation -- "proof valid + thumbprint" --> IntentVerification
   135→    DPoPValidation -- "proof valid + thumbprint" --> BridgeExchange
   136→    IntentVerification -- "intent verified" --> TokenIssuance
   137→    BridgeExchange -- "session valid + intent authorized" --> TokenIssuance
   138→    TokenIssuance -- "reads key" --> SigningKeyStore
   139→```
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→## C4 Component (Level 3) -- Brain Resource Server DPoP Verification
   144→
   145→```mermaid
   146→graph TB
   147→    subgraph ResourceServer["Brain Resource Server Verification Pipeline"]
   148→        direction TB
   149→
   150→        HeaderExtraction["Header Extraction\n- Authorization: DPoP <token>\n- DPoP: <proof>"]
   151→
   152→        TokenValidation["Access Token Validation\n- JWT signature (AS public key)\n- Expiry check\n- Extract cnf.jkt\n- Extract authorization_details"]
   153→
   154→        ProofValidation["DPoP Proof Validation\n- Structure (typ=dpop+jwt, alg=ES256)\n- Signature verification\n- htm matches HTTP method\n- htu matches request URI\n- iat within clock skew window"]
   155→
   156→        ReplayCheck["Replay Protection\n- jti uniqueness check\n- Time-windowed nonce cache\n- Auto-expiry of entries"]
   157→
   158→        SenderBinding["Sender Binding\n- Compute proof JWK thumbprint\n- Compare against cnf.jkt\n- Log security event on mismatch"]
   159→
   160→        OperationScope["Operation Scope Verification\n- Extract requested brain_action\n  from route + body\n- Match type, action, resource\n- Verify constraint bounds"]
   161→    end
   162→
   163→    Request["Incoming Request"] --> HeaderExtraction
   164→    HeaderExtraction -- "token + proof" --> TokenValidation
   165→    TokenValidation -- "cnf.jkt extracted" --> ProofValidation
   166→    ProofValidation -- "proof valid" --> ReplayCheck
   167→    ReplayCheck -- "jti unique" --> SenderBinding
   168→    SenderBinding -- "thumbprint matches" --> OperationScope
   169→    OperationScope -- "authorized" --> RouteHandler["Route Handler (existing)"]
   170→```
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→## Integration Points with Existing System
   175→
   176→### Modified Components
   177→
   178→| Component | Current | After |
   179→|---|---|---|
   180→| `mcp/auth.ts` (`authenticateMcpRequest`) | Bearer token extraction, JWKS validation, scope extraction | Replaced by DPoP verification pipeline. Bearer requests rejected with 401 "dpop_required". |
   181→| `auth/scopes.ts` (`requireScope`, `ACTION_SCOPE_MAP`) | Gates MCP operations by OAuth scope | Removed from Brain boundary. Scopes remain for Better Auth UI only. |
   182→| `mcp/mcp-route.ts` | Calls `authenticateMcpRequest` + `requireScope` per handler | Calls new DPoP+RAR verification middleware. Scope checks removed. |
   183→| `intent/types.ts` (`IntentRecord`) | `action_spec` with provider/action/params | Extended with `authorization_details` (brain_action), `dpop_jwk_thumbprint` |
   184→| `intent/authorizer.ts` (`evaluateIntent`) | Evaluates action_spec | Evaluates brain_action authorization_details (Rich Intent Objects) |
   185→| `intent/intent-routes.ts` | Evaluation webhook handler, veto, list pending | Extended with consent rendering data |
   186→| `mcp/token-validation.ts` | JWKS-based Bearer JWT validation | Repurposed for AS-issued DPoP-bound token validation |
   187→| `runtime/types.ts` (`ServerDependencies`) | No nonce cache | Adds nonce cache and AS signing key |
   188→| `start-server.ts` | Routes for MCP, intents | Adds Custom AS token endpoint, Bridge endpoint |
   189→
   190→### New Components
   191→
   192→| Component | Responsibility |
   193→|---|---|
   194→| `app/src/server/oauth/dpop.ts` | DPoP proof construction/validation, JWK thumbprint computation |
   195→| `app/src/server/oauth/token-endpoint.ts` | Custom AS token endpoint (grant_type=urn:brain:intent-authorization) |
   196→| `app/src/server/oauth/bridge.ts` | Bridge exchange endpoint (session -> DPoP token) |
   197→| `app/src/server/oauth/token-issuer.ts` | JWT signing with brain_action claims, cnf.jkt |
   198→| `app/src/server/oauth/nonce-cache.ts` | Time-windowed nonce set for replay protection |
   199→| `app/src/server/oauth/rar-verifier.ts` | brain_action matching (route -> requested action -> authorized action) |
   200→| `app/src/server/oauth/dpop-middleware.ts` | Request verification pipeline (replaces authenticateMcpRequest) |
   201→| `app/src/server/oauth/consent-renderer.ts` | brain_action to human-readable display |
   202→| `app/src/server/oauth/audit.ts` | Authorization event logging |
   203→| `app/src/server/oauth/types.ts` | Shared types (BrainAction, DPoP claims, token claims) |
   204→
   205→### Preserved Components (No Changes)
   206→
   207→- `auth/config.ts` -- Better Auth configuration (human login unchanged)
   208→- `auth/adapter.ts` -- SurrealDB adapter for Better Auth
   209→- `iam/authority.ts` -- Authority permission matrix (coexists, gates tool execution)
   210→- `iam/identity.ts` -- Identity resolution (unchanged)
   211→- `intent/risk-router.ts` -- Risk routing logic (unchanged)
   212→- `intent/status-machine.ts` -- Intent state machine (unchanged)
   213→- `intent/veto-manager.ts` -- Veto window recovery (unchanged)
   214→- All chat, extraction, entity, graph, feed, observation modules -- unchanged
   215→
   216→---
   217→
   218→## Deployment Architecture
   219→
   220→Single Bun process (modular monolith). The Custom AS is a new module within the existing server, not a separate service.
   221→
   222→```
   223→Brain Server (Bun process)
   224→  |
   225→  +-- Better Auth Plugin (existing, human login)
   226→  |     |-- /api/auth/* routes (sessions, OAuth provider, JWKS)
   227→  |
   228→  +-- Custom Authorization Server (NEW module)
   229→  |     |-- POST /api/auth/intents (intent submission with DPoP binding)
   230→  |     |-- POST /api/auth/token (RAR token issuance)
   231→  |     |-- POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange (session-to-token Bridge)
   232→  |     |-- GET  /api/auth/brain/.well-known/jwks (AS public keys)
   233→  |
   234→  +-- Brain Resource Server (MODIFIED middleware)
   235→  |     |-- DPoP verification pipeline (replaces Bearer auth)
   236→  |     |-- RAR operation scope verification
   237→  |     |-- /api/mcp/* routes (existing, now DPoP-protected)
   238→  |     |-- /api/workspaces/* routes (existing, now DPoP-protected)
   239→  |
   240→  +-- Intent Evaluation Pipeline (existing, extended)
   241→  |     |-- Policy Gate -> Authorizer Agent -> Risk Router
   242→  |     |-- Now evaluates brain_action (not action_spec)
   243→  |
   244→  +-- SurrealDB (graph database)
   245→```
   246→
   247→---
   248→
   249→## Data Flow: Agent Token Acquisition
   250→
   251→```
   252→1. Agent generates ES256 key pair (Web Crypto API, in-memory)
   253→2. Agent computes JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638)
   254→3. Agent constructs brain_action authorization_details
   255→4. Agent submits intent:
   256→     POST /api/auth/intents
   257→     { authorization_details, dpop_jwk_thumbprint, goal, reasoning }
   258→5. Evaluation pipeline runs:
   259→     Policy Gate -> Authorizer Agent (LLM) -> Risk Router
   260→6. If auto_approve: intent.status = "authorized"
   261→   If veto_window: human reviews, approves/constrains/vetoes
   262→7. Agent requests token:
   263→     POST /api/auth/token
   264→     Headers: DPoP: <proof signed with agent's private key>
   265→     Body: { grant_type: "urn:brain:intent-authorization", intent_id, authorization_details }
   266→8. Custom AS validates:
   267→     - Intent exists, status = authorized
   268→     - DPoP proof valid (structure, signature, freshness)
   269→     - Proof key thumbprint matches intent.dpop_jwk_thumbprint
   270→     - authorization_details matches intent
   271→9. Custom AS issues DPoP-bound access token:
   272→     { sub, cnf: { jkt: thumbprint }, authorization_details, urn:brain:intent_id, exp }
   273→10. Agent presents token to Brain:
   274→      Authorization: DPoP <token>
   275→      DPoP: <fresh proof for this request>
   276→```
   277→
   278→## Data Flow: Human Bridge Exchange
   279→
   280→```
   281→1. Human logs into dashboard via Better Auth (session cookie)
   282→2. Dashboard generates ES256 key pair (Web Crypto API, non-extractable)
   283→3. Dashboard constructs brain_action for the requested operation
   284→4. Dashboard sends Bridge exchange:
   285→     POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange
   286→     Cookie: better-auth session
   287→     Headers: DPoP: <proof signed with browser key>
   288→     Body: { authorization_details }
   289→5. Custom AS validates:
   290→     - Better Auth session is active (API call)
   291→     - DPoP proof valid
   292→     - Resolves human identity from session
   293→     - Authorizer Agent evaluates brain_action
   294→     - Low-risk reads auto-approve; high-risk triggers veto window
   295→6. Custom AS issues DPoP-bound access token (same format as agent tokens)
   296→7. Dashboard presents DPoP token to Brain (same verification pipeline)
   297→```
   298→
   299→---
   300→
   301→## Quality Attribute Strategies
   302→
   303→### Security
   304→- DPoP sender constraining eliminates stolen token replay
   305→- Session cookies cannot access Brain (enforced at middleware)
   306→- Bearer tokens rejected at Brain boundary
   307→- Nonce cache prevents DPoP proof replay
   308→- Short token TTL (300s default)
   309→- brain_action constraints enforce operation bounds
   310→- AS signing key: ES256 key pair generated on first server start, persisted to SurrealDB (`as_signing_key` table), loaded from DB on subsequent starts. Key rotation via new key insertion + old key status = "rotated" (JWKS serves both during transition).
   311→- Rate limiting on token/Bridge endpoints: deferred (opportunity score 8.0, overserved). The intent evaluation pipeline and DPoP proof validation provide natural rate control. Add per-identity rate limiting if operational data shows abuse.
   312→
   313→### Auditability
   314→- Every intent submission, evaluation, routing, consent, token issuance, and verification logged
   315→- Audit entries link to intent_id + DPoP thumbprint
   316→- Security events (mismatch, replay) at elevated severity
   317→- Uniform brain_action format for all actors
   318→
   319→### Testability
   320→- Evaluation pipeline uses injected LlmEvaluator port (existing pattern)
   321→- DPoP proof validation is pure function (input: proof JWT, output: validation result)
   322→- Nonce cache is dependency-injected (not module singleton)
   323→- Token issuance uses injected signing key
   324→- Brain_action matching is pure function (requested vs authorized)
   325→
   326→### Maintainability
   327→- New `oauth/` module with clear boundaries
   328→- Pure functions for validation, matching, thumbprint computation
   329→- Effect boundary at endpoint handlers only
   330→- Existing intent pipeline extended, not rewritten
   331→
   332→### Time-to-Market
   333→- Reuses existing intent table, evaluation pipeline, risk router, identity system
   334→- `jose` library already in dependencies for JWT/JWK operations
   335→- Web Crypto API available in Bun (no new dependencies for key generation)
   336→- Single module addition, not architectural restructuring
   337→

roadmap:
  project_id: oauth-rar-dpop
  created_at: '2026-03-10T09:46:23Z'
  total_steps: 13
  phases: 4
phases:
- id: '01'
  name: 'Foundation: Types, Pure DPoP, Schema'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'OAuth shared types and DPoP pure functions'
    description: >
      Define algebraic data types (BrainAction, DPoPProofClaims, DPoPBoundTokenClaims,
      discriminated unions for validation/issuance results). Implement pure DPoP proof
      validation (structure, signature, claims) and JWK thumbprint computation (RFC 7638)
      using jose library and Web Crypto API.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
    - 'ES256 key pair generated with computable JWK thumbprint'
    - 'Thumbprint is deterministic for same public key, unique across key pairs'
    - 'DPoP proof JWT validated for structure (typ, alg, jwk), signature, and claims (htm, htu, iat, jti)'
    - 'Clock skew tolerance: 60s past, 5s future'
    - 'Enables: M1-K1 (key pair generation), M1-K2 (performance), M1-K3 (deterministic thumbprint), M1-K4 (unique thumbprints)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/types.ts
    - app/src/server/oauth/dpop.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/types.test.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/dpop.test.ts
  - id: 01-02
    name: 'Nonce cache for replay protection'
    description: >
      Time-windowed nonce set storing seen jti values with timestamps.
      Factory function returns cache instance for dependency injection.
      Auto-expires entries beyond clock skew window.
    time_estimate: 2h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
    - 'Nonce cache rejects previously seen jti within time window'
    - 'Expired entries auto-purged beyond clock skew window'
    - 'Factory returns injectable instance (no module singleton)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/nonce-cache.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/nonce-cache.test.ts
  - id: 01-03
    name: 'Schema migration for OAuth fields and tables'
    description: >
      Extend intent table with authorization_details, dpop_jwk_thumbprint,
      token_issued_at, token_expires_at. Define audit_event and as_signing_key
      tables. Add identity table fields for managed_by, status, revoked_at.
    time_estimate: 2h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
    - 'Intent table accepts authorization_details (array of objects) and dpop_jwk_thumbprint (string)'
    - 'audit_event table stores event_type, severity, actor, workspace, intent_id, dpop_thumbprint, payload'
    - 'as_signing_key table stores kid, algorithm, public_jwk, private_jwk, status'
    - 'Identity table supports managed_by, status, revoked_at fields'
    - 'Migration applies atomically via bun migrate'
    implementation_scope:
    - schema/migrations/0022_oauth_rar_dpop.surql
    - schema/surreal-schema.surql
- id: '02'
  name: 'Custom Authorization Server'
  steps:
  - id: 02-01
    name: 'Token issuer with DPoP binding'
    description: >
      Sign DPoP-bound access tokens with Custom AS ES256 key. Token includes
      sub, cnf.jkt, authorization_details, urn:brain:intent_id, urn:brain:workspace,
      exp. Default TTL 300s. Signing key injected via ServerDependencies.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-01]
    criteria:
    - 'Issued token contains cnf.jkt matching actor thumbprint'
    - 'Token embeds authorization_details array and urn:brain:intent_id claim'
    - 'Token TTL is at most 300 seconds'
    - 'Token signed with AS ES256 key, verifiable via AS public key'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/token-issuer.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/token-issuer.test.ts
  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Intent submission with DPoP thumbprint binding'
    description: >
      Extend intent creation endpoint (POST /api/auth/intents) to require
      authorization_details with type "brain_action" and dpop_jwk_thumbprint.
      Validate input, store fields, trigger existing evaluation pipeline.
      Reject missing thumbprint, missing authorization_details, wrong type.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: [01-01, 01-03]
    criteria:
    - 'Intent accepted with brain_action authorization_details and thumbprint stored in record'
    - 'Missing thumbprint rejected with 400 and clear error'
    - 'Missing or wrong-type authorization_details rejected with 400'
    - 'Low-risk read intents auto-approve without human intervention'
    - 'Enables: M1-K5 (key reuse), M1-K6 (submit with binding), M1-K7 (no thumbprint), M1-K8 (no auth details), M1-K9 (wrong type), M1-K10 (auto-approve), M1-K11 (constraints preserved)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts
    - app/src/server/intent/types.ts
    - app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts
    - app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts
  - id: 02-03
    name: 'Custom AS token endpoint'
    description: >
      POST /api/auth/token accepting grant_type=urn:brain:intent-authorization.
      Validates DPoP proof, verifies intent status=authorized, matches proof key
      thumbprint to intent binding, checks authorization_details match. Delegates
      to token issuer. Supports re-issuance for same intent.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: [02-01, 02-02]
    criteria:
    - 'Authorized intent receives DPoP-bound access token with token_type=DPoP'
    - 'Token rejected for non-authorized intent status (pending, vetoed)'
    - 'Token rejected when proof key thumbprint does not match intent binding'
    - 'Token rejected when requested authorization_details do not match intent'
    - 'Token rejected for missing DPoP proof or non-existent intent'
    - 'Enables: M2-K1 through M2-K9 (all token issuance scenarios), Walking Skeleton 1 (steps 4-5)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/token-endpoint.ts
    - app/src/server/oauth/audit.ts
    - app/src/server/runtime/types.ts
    - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
  - id: 02-04
    name: 'AS signing key bootstrap and management'
    description: >
      Generate ES256 key pair on first server start, persist to as_signing_key
      table. Load from DB on subsequent starts. Expose JWKS endpoint
      (GET /api/auth/brain/.well-known/jwks). Inject into ServerDependencies.
    time_estimate: 2h
    dependencies: [01-03, 02-01]
    criteria:
    - 'AS signing key generated on first start and persisted to SurrealDB'
    - 'Subsequent starts load existing key from DB'
    - 'JWKS endpoint returns AS public key in JWK Set format'
    - 'asSigningKey available in ServerDependencies for token issuer'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/as-key-management.ts
    - app/src/server/runtime/dependencies.ts
    - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
- id: '03'
  name: 'Brain Resource Server Verification'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'DPoP verification middleware'
    description: >
      Brain resource server pipeline replacing authenticateMcpRequest. Extracts
      Authorization: DPoP + DPoP headers. Rejects Bearer tokens, session cookies,
      missing auth with 401 dpop_required. Validates token signature via AS JWKS,
      validates proof, checks nonce cache, verifies sender binding (cnf.jkt match).
    time_estimate: 5h
    dependencies: [01-01, 01-02, 02-04]
    criteria:
    - 'Bearer tokens rejected with 401 and dpop_required error'
    - 'Session cookies rejected with 401 and dpop_required error'
    - 'Missing authentication rejected with 401'
    - 'Valid DPoP proof with matching token grants access'
    - 'Proof with wrong method, wrong URI, or different key rejected with 401'
    - 'Enables: M3-K1 (reject Bearer), M3-K2 (reject cookies), M3-K3 (reject none), M3-K4 (valid proof), M3-K5 (wrong method), M3-K6 (wrong URI), M3-K7 (different key)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/dpop-middleware.ts
    - app/src/server/mcp/auth.ts
  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Replay protection and clock skew enforcement'
    description: >
      Integrate nonce cache into DPoP middleware for jti replay detection.
      Enforce clock skew tolerance (60s past, 5s future) on proof iat.
      Reject proofs with missing JWK in header.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [03-01]
    criteria:
    - 'Replayed DPoP proof (same jti) rejected on second use'
    - 'Proof with iat beyond 60s in the past rejected'
    - 'Proof with iat beyond 5s in the future rejected'
    - 'Proof with missing JWK in header rejected'
    - 'Enables: M3-K8 (replay), M3-K9 (clock past), M3-K10 (clock future), M3-K11 (missing JWK)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/dpop-middleware.ts
    - app/src/server/oauth/nonce-cache.ts
  - id: 03-03
    name: 'RAR operation scope verifier and route-action map'
    description: >
      Map HTTP method+path to brain_action via route-action-map. Match
      requested operation against token authorization_details: type, action,
      resource exact match; constraint bounds enforcement (numeric <=).
      Return specific error codes: authorization_details_mismatch,
      authorization_params_exceeded.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: [01-01]
    criteria:
    - 'Request matching token action and resource succeeds'
    - 'Request for different action than authorized rejected with 403 authorization_details_mismatch'
    - 'Request exceeding authorized constraints rejected with 403 authorization_params_exceeded'
    - 'Enables: M3-K12 (scope match), M3-K13 (scope mismatch), M3-K14 (params exceeded)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/rar-verifier.ts
    - app/src/server/oauth/route-action-map.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/rar-verifier.test.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/route-action-map.test.ts
  - id: 03-04
    name: 'MCP route migration to DPoP + RAR'
    description: >
      Replace authenticateMcpRequest + requireScope in mcp-route.ts with
      DPoP verification middleware + RAR verifier. Uniform pipeline for
      all actor types. Walking Skeleton 1 fully passes after this step.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [03-01, 03-02, 03-03]
    criteria:
    - 'All MCP routes use DPoP verification instead of Bearer auth'
    - 'Agent and human tokens verified through identical pipeline'
    - 'Walking Skeleton 1 (agent path) passes end-to-end'
    - 'Enables: M3-K15 (uniform pipeline), Walking Skeleton 1 complete'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts
    - app/src/server/mcp/auth.ts
- id: '04'
  name: 'Bridge Exchange + Integration'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'Bridge session-to-token exchange'
    description: >
      POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange accepting Better Auth session cookie +
      DPoP proof + authorization_details. Validates session, resolves human
      identity, creates implicit intent, evaluates via pipeline (auto-approve
      low-risk reads, veto window for high-risk). Issues DPoP-bound token.
      Walking Skeleton 2 passes after this step.
    time_estimate: 5h
    dependencies: [02-01, 02-03, 03-01]
    criteria:
    - 'Valid session + DPoP proof + brain_action yields DPoP-bound token'
    - 'Bridge token contains correct cnf.jkt and authorization_details'
    - 'Low-risk reads auto-approve; high-risk triggers evaluation'
    - 'Expired/invalid session rejected with 401'
    - 'Missing session, missing DPoP proof, or missing authorization_details rejected'
    - 'Enables: M4-K1 through M4-K9 (all Bridge scenarios), Walking Skeleton 2 complete'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/bridge.ts
    - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
  - id: 04-02
    name: 'Consent renderer, managed identity, audit wiring'
    description: >
      Consent renderer transforms brain_action to human-readable display
      (provider-specific formatting, amounts in dollars). Consent endpoints
      for approve/veto/constrain actions. Managed agent identity with
      managed_by relationship, revocation checks at intent submission and
      Brain verification. Audit event logging wired to all auth operations.
    time_estimate: 5h
    dependencies: [02-02, 03-04, 04-01]
    criteria:
    - 'brain_action rendered with human-readable labels and formatted values (amounts in dollars)'
    - 'Human can approve, veto (with reason), or constrain (tighter bounds only) pending intents'
    - 'Constrain rejects looser bounds than original authorization'
    - 'Managed agent identity records managed_by; revoked identity rejected at submission and Brain boundary'
    - 'Enables: M5-K1 through M5-K8 (all consent and identity scenarios)'
    implementation_scope:
    - app/src/server/oauth/consent-renderer.ts
    - app/src/server/oauth/audit.ts
    - app/src/server/intent/intent-routes.ts
    - tests/unit/oauth/consent-renderer.test.ts
implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
  - app/src/server/oauth/
  - app/src/server/intent/
  - app/src/server/mcp/
  - app/src/server/runtime/
  test_directories:
  - tests/acceptance/oauth-rar-dpop/
  - tests/unit/oauth/
  excluded_patterns:
  - node_modules/**
  - dist/**

Review roadmap: /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/lusaka/docs/feature/oauth-rar-dpop/roadmap.yaml

This is the implementation roadmap for OAuth 2.1 RAR + DPoP sovereign authorization server for the Brain platform.

Context for your review:
- This is a TypeScript/Bun functional paradigm project
- Acceptance tests already exist at tests/acceptance/oauth-rar-dpop/ (56 scenarios across 7 files)
- The roadmap must guide Outside-In TDD implementation using those acceptance tests
- The dependency graph flows: types (leaf) → pure functions (dpop, nonce-cache, rar-verifier) → effect shells (token-endpoint, bridge, dpop-middleware)
- Walking Skeleton 1 (agent path) should pass after MCP route migration, Walking Skeleton 2 (human Bridge path) after Bridge exchange

Review dimensions:
1. Step decomposition (atomic, self-contained steps with clear boundaries)
2. Dependency ordering (correct topological sort, no circular deps)
3. Acceptance criteria quality (behavioral, measurable, tied to test scenarios)
4. Implementation scope accuracy (correct files, no missing files)
5. Walking skeleton enablement order (do the walking skeletons pass at the right steps?)
6. Risk assessment (are the hardest/riskiest steps identified?)

Use Conventional Comments format. Priority-order findings: blocking issues first, then suggestions, then praise.

Update the roadmap.yaml validation section with your review verdict (APPROVED, NEEDS_REVISION, or REJECTED).
